data.body: CONCLUSION (AC2): SCORER HEALTHY, and the zero-drop premise was a measurement artifact. Decision rule applied: (a) live-call error/empty rate 0% (threshold 20%) - not defective; (b) carryovers score < 0.7 in 92/92 cases - not defective. Crucially, re-examining the eligible sidecars with a corrected parser (some REVIEW_RESULT events store data as a JSON-encoded string; the original scan silently skipped those) shows the production floor HAS been dropping: 33/35 eligible reviews have coverage.narrowed=true with 1-9 floored findings each (~111 total, matching the probe's 115 predicted within round-composition noise), drop_reason=novelty persisted. Sidecar examples: ticket 9d50-ead1-de68-4362 events 1783885570421461001 and 1783886442583379001 (probe drop-quadrant findings, production-floored same rounds); ticket 0730-6ceb-88c3-430c event of 2026-07-15 (round-2 review: eligible=true, narrowed=true, 5 findings floored with drop_reason). The REAL remaining gap is unchanged: BLOCK-loop rounds are never eligible (signed=False), which story a850-1392-55b0-4514 fixes. No scorer-repair bug filed (AC4: healthy). The non-live-runnable eval gap is bug ba85-4149-8049-45a7 (AC3, discovered_from linked).
